evals.name: basic-v6-migration
evals.prompt: Our company can't stay on FluentAssertions because of the new Xceed commercial licensing, so we're moving to AwesomeAssertions. Please migrate the xunit test project InventoryApp.Tests (in this directory) from FluentAssertions 6.12 to the latest AwesomeAssertions. When you're done the project must build cleanly and all tests must pass.
evals.expected_output: Project references latest AwesomeAssertions (no FluentAssertions package), namespaces renamed to AwesomeAssertions, renamed APIs fixed (HaveCountGreaterOrEqualTo -> HaveCountGreaterThanOrEqualTo, BeLessOrEqualTo -> BeLessThanOrEqualTo, EquivalencyAssertionOptions -> EquivalencyOptions, RespectingRuntimeTypes -> PreferringRuntimeMemberTypes, ExcludingNestedObjects -> WithoutRecursing, AssertionOptions -> AssertionConfiguration.Current), builds with 0 errors, all 9 tests pass.
assertions: No FluentAssertions package reference remains in any project file
Project references the AwesomeAssertions NuGet package, major version 9 or later
No FluentAssertions identifier remains anywhere in the C# sources (usings, fully-qualified names, global usings)
dotnet build succeeds with 0 errors
dotnet test discovers 9 tests and all 9 pass (none removed, renamed away, or skipped)

Removed v7 API names are gone: HaveCountGreaterOrEqualTo, HaveCountLessOrEqualTo, BeGreaterOrEqualTo, BeLessOrEqualTo, EquivalencyAssertionOptions, RespectingRuntimeTypes, ExcludingNestedObjects, AssertionOptions
Test semantics preserved: every original assertion still checks the same condition (nothing deleted or weakened to force a pass)

evals.name: custom-assertions-v7-migration
evals.prompt: We're replacing FluentAssertions 7 with AwesomeAssertions (latest) in ShippingService.Tests (in this directory). The tricky part: we have our own custom assertion classes built on the FluentAssertions extensibility API - ShipmentAssertions, a StringAssertions extension, a custom value formatter and a custom equivalency step. Migrate the whole project including those custom assertions. All existing tests must still pass afterwards.
evals.expected_output: Project references latest AwesomeAssertions, namespaces renamed, custom assertions rewritten on the AssertionChain API (no Execute.Assertion left, constructors thread AssertionChain, WithExpectation nested lambda instead of ClearExpectation, AndWhichConstraint receives the chain, IEquivalencyStep uses IValidateChildNodeEquivalency / EquivalencyProven, AssertionOptions.EquivalencyPlan -> AssertionConfiguration.Current.Equivalency.Plan), builds with 0 errors, all 9 tests pass.
assertions: No FluentAssertions package reference remains; AwesomeAssertions major version 9+ is referenced
dotnet build succeeds with 0 errors
dotnet test discovers 9 tests and all 9 pass
No Execute.Assertion calls remain anywhere
Custom assertion classes thread AssertionChain: Should() calls AssertionChain.GetOrCreate() and the constructor passes the chain to base
WithExpectation uses the nested-lambda form and ClearExpectation is gone
ContainItem returns AndWhichConstraint constructed WITH the assertion chain (not the 2-argument v7 form)
Equivalency step uses the IValidateChildNodeEquivalency parameter type and EquivalencyResult.EquivalencyProven, and is registered via AssertionConfiguration.Current.Equivalency.Plan
Test semantics preserved: no assertion deleted or weakened to force a pass

evals.name: fa8-central-packages-rename
evals.prompt: we're on FluentAssertions 8.10 and don't want to pay for the Xceed license, so switch us over to the latest AwesomeAssertions. this solution (in this directory) uses central package management via Directory.Packages.props. there's a shared TestKit class library with custom assertions and the ApiClient.Tests test project. everything should build and the tests should pass when you're done
evals.expected_output: Directory.Packages.props references latest AwesomeAssertions instead of FluentAssertions, both projects' namespaces renamed FluentAssertions -> AwesomeAssertions (the v8-style AssertionChain code needs no structural changes), builds with 0 errors, all 5 tests pass.
assertions: Directory.Packages.props references AwesomeAssertions (latest) and contains no FluentAssertions entry
Both projects build with 0 errors
dotnet test discovers 5 tests and all 5 pass
No FluentAssertions identifier remains in any source or project file
Migration is minimal: the v8-style AssertionChain code was only namespace-renamed, not structurally rewritten
Test semantics preserved: no assertion deleted or weakened to force a pass

evals.name: legacy-v5-multiproject
evals.prompt: This solution is stuck on FluentAssertions 5.10.3 and .NET 5, and we need off FluentAssertions for licensing reasons. Migrate all three projects (LabelKit, TestSupport, LabelKit.Tests - in this directory) to the latest AwesomeAssertions, and since .NET 5 is long out of support, retarget the net5.0/net50 projects to net8.0 while you're at it. Watch out: we have a custom assertions class for our label tokens plus a shared assertion extension in TestSupport, both built on the old FluentAssertions internals. Everything must build and all tests must pass at the end.
evals.expected_output: All projects reference latest AwesomeAssertions (no FluentAssertions), namespaces renamed, test/support projects retargeted to net8.0 (including the 'net50' typo TFM), v5-style custom assertions rewritten on AssertionChain (Subject assignment via base ctor, Execute.Assertion gone, ObjectAssertions extension uses CurrentAssertionChain), v5 renames applied (BeGreaterOrEqualTo -> BeGreaterThanOrEqualTo etc.), builds 0 errors, all 6 tests pass.
assertions: No FluentAssertions package reference remains; TestSupport and LabelKit.Tests reference AwesomeAssertions major version 9+
Legacy TFMs are gone: no net5.0 or net50 remains; test and support projects target net8.0
All projects build with 0 errors
dotnet test discovers 6 tests and all 6 pass
No Execute.Assertion calls remain anywhere
LabelTokenAssertions threads AssertionChain (GetOrCreate in Should(), chain passed to base ctor instead of v5-style Subject assignment)
The shared ObjectAssertions extension obtains the chain via CurrentAssertionChain (or equivalent), not a static entry point
Renamed APIs adopted: no BeGreaterOrEqualTo / BeLessOrEqualTo remain
No FluentAssertions identifier remains in sources or project files
Test semantics preserved: every original assertion still checks the same condition (nothing deleted or weakened to force a pass)

evals.name: sensor-v5-wrapper-class
evals.prompt: migrate SensorFeed.Tests (in this directory) from FluentAssertions 5.10.3 to the latest AwesomeAssertions. also bump the project from net5.0 to net8.0, .net 5 isn't even installed on our build agents anymore. heads up: there's a WindowCheck helper class that calls into FluentAssertions internals (Execute.Assertion with Then-chaining), and the tests use the fluent date helpers (21.March(2024) etc). all tests must pass when you're done
evals.expected_output: Project references latest AwesomeAssertions, net8.0 target, WindowCheck rewritten on AssertionChain (Execute.Assertion gone, Then-chaining preserved), fluent date extensions keep working via AwesomeAssertions.Extensions, BeGreaterOrEqualTo/BeLessOrEqualTo renamed, builds 0 errors, all 6 tests pass.
assertions: No FluentAssertions package reference remains; AwesomeAssertions major version 9+ is referenced
Project targets net8.0 (no net5.0 remains)
dotnet build succeeds with 0 errors
dotnet test discovers 6 tests and all 6 pass
No Execute.Assertion calls remain; WindowCheck uses AssertionChain
Renamed APIs adopted: no BeGreaterOrEqualTo / BeLessOrEqualTo remain
No FluentAssertions identifier remains in sources or project files (fluent dates via AwesomeAssertions.Extensions)
Test semantics preserved: every original assertion still checks the same condition (nothing deleted or weakened to force a pass)
